prompt:
A close-up of a single Benchy model on a 3D printer’s build plate being used for retraction tuning. The Benchy in the foreground shows minimal stringing and clean details, while in the background a small row of earlier test Benchies with visible stringing and wispy filament strands are lined up on the workbench. A laptop or printer screen off to the side shows a simple icon-only slider graphic, suggesting adjustments to retraction settings without any readable text.

Near-future solarpunk workshop aesthetic with warm lighting, tidy cables, and a plant or two on shelves. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering, no brand logos or legible UI text. The composition should make it obvious that the user is printing Benchy specifically to tune retraction and reduce stringing.
